title: Virtualization, Containers, VDI, and Isolation Boundaries · Causal Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs virtualization, containers, vdi, and isolation boundaries from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for virtual workload placement and evidence plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, VDI, and remote sessions.
Match CPU extensions, cores, memory, storage, graphics, network, firmware, operating system, licensing, and workload concurrency to host capacity.
Trace virtual adapters, switches, modes, addresses, routes, shared folders, virtual disks, thin provisioning, snapshots, and underlying failure domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Separate evidence from confidence · Review Virtual Fault Evidence
steps.instruction:
review case — A lab treats VMs, containers, snapshots, VDI, device passthrough, and disposable sandboxes as equivalent isolation and recovery mechanisms. The review variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests.

Inspect the Virtual Fault Evidence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Separate evidence from confidence · Review Virtual Fault Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests. For CompTIA A+ support Separate evidence from confidence · Review Virtual Fault Evidence, keep Virtual Fault Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

stimulus.title: Virtual Fault Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A lab treats VMs, containers, snapshots, VDI, device passthrough, and disposable sandboxes as equivalent isolation and recovery mechanisms. The review variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Restarting the guest proves the defect was inside the guest operating system.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests. Artifact under review: virtual workload placement and evidence plan.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim fails the evidence boundary for Virtual Fault Evidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply one familiar repair, omit user impact, authorization, safety, asset and version identity, baseline, recent change, competing cause, prediction, observation, rollback, changed-case verification, accessibility, privacy, escalation, and controlled transfer, then accept a missing symptom as support proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Virtual Fault Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
